hub: kosha (Gasuns Sanskrit Dictionary) — the Sanskrit data-hub
note_for_agents: This is the ONE machine-readable list of canonical derived Sanskrit datasets across the ~85 repos. Before deriving an alignment/frequency/headword/crosswalk asset, check here. tier=public assets are downloadable from the interim_release URL; tier=restricted assets exist but are not published (rights-encumbered or unbackuped local-only) — consult their source repo; tier=intermediate is raw/working data that feeds another registered dataset — not standalone-citable, not rights-gated, just not a finished asset in its own right (excluded from the public directory page). A session that creates or changes a derived dataset MUST add/update its row here in the same pass.

datasets.title: MW verbal-root inventory
datasets.keying: SLP1 root + explicit verb_type (750 genuineroot + 1363 root)
datasets.notes: 3 repos independently re-scanned mw.txt before this existed (got 750/809/1163) — consume this, never re-scan.

datasets.title: Headword→root derivation table (Pāṇinian, 10 dicts)
datasets.keying: SLP1 headword → root
datasets.notes: Derivations, distinct from the root inventory (mw-roots).

datasets.title: DCS lemma ↔ CDSL headword crosswalk
datasets.keying: DCS lemma id ↔ CDSL headword (SLP1), 81.4% linked
datasets.notes: Reusable LOD linkset — never re-derive DCS↔CDSL joins.

datasets.title: Cross-dictionary union headword index
datasets.keying: SLP1 headword + per-dict provenance flags
datasets.notes: THE headword master — 323k headwords across all dicts.

datasets.title: MW ↔ Heritage (INRIA) entry-level crosswalk
datasets.keying: MW entry id → Heritage anchor (25,140 covered, 97.6% anchor-resolved)
consumers: kosha ingest (H345, kosha.db heritage_anchor table + /api/v1/lemma `heritage` witness)
datasets.notes: Crosswalk itself is ours/publishable; the Heritage mirror data behind it is LGPLLR-pending (restricted tier).

datasets.title: DCS lemma frequency sidecar (whole-corpus + per-period)
datasets.keying: SLP1 lemma; whole-corpus counts + per-period vectors + core-vocab coverage
datasets.builder: kosha DCS M9 join against union_headwords
datasets.notes: LEFT-JOIN this — never re-derive frequency ordering. ⚠ upstream UD Tense=Past conflates aorist/perfect.

datasets.title: Zaliznyak-style compact grammar-token index over all PWG
datasets.keying: SLP1 headword → paradigm token (335 tokens)
datasets.notes: FAIR data package; paradigms generated via vidyut, never transcribe static tables.

datasets.title: Sa→Ru word-aligned corpus lexicon
datasets.keying: SLP1 surface key (~190k keys), per verse-pair alignment
datasets.notes: RIGHTS: aligns PUBLISHED Russian translations — not publishable as-is. BACKUP GAP: registered asset existing in exactly one place. P-D2/H233. Live count 11-07-2026: 1,093,391 rows (append-only, still growing); the ONLY genuinely single-copy giant in the census §2 list per the H691 sweep.
datasets.rebuild: NOT deterministically rebuildable — DeepSeek LLM alignment (RussianTranslation/src/build_corpus_lexicon.py, append-only, resumable) over the SamudraManthanam corpus; a re-run costs weeks + API spend and yields different output. SINGLE COPY on MG disk — backup pending H235.

datasets.title: Ranked Sa→Ru glossary (surface/lemma/root layers)
datasets.keying: SLP1; surface 190,838 · lemma 40,370 · root 2,021; 87% token coverage
datasets.source_path: bulk layers gitignored; ranked site data tracked
datasets.notes: Derived from corpus-lexicon → same rights caveat for bulk layers. Site-served ranked subset is public. H691: surface layer verified 190,838 lines, sha256 recorded; backup exists (tracked in gasyoun/SanskritRussian), so no census backup-gap for this asset.
datasets.rebuild: Rebuildable from corpus-lexicon via RussianTranslation/src/build_surface_glossary.py (+ lemma/root siblings); bulk layers are ALSO git-tracked in gasyoun/SanskritRussian (surface_glossary.jsonl.gz, lemma_glossary.jsonl, root_glossary.jsonl) — backed up, not single-copy.

datasets.title: kosha unified lookup database
datasets.keying: 8 tables: 444,773 entries · 323,425 lemmas · 692,403 senses · 1,378,401 forms · 6,916,522 inflections (+ meta, sources, stem_bridge)
datasets.source_path: data/db/kosha.db (GITIGNORED — regenerable via scripts/build_db.py chain, but hours of compute)
datasets.notes: Regenerable but expensive; backup = cheap insurance. Candidate single queryable DB for P-D5. ⚠ H691: live DB (built 05-07-2026, verified 11-07-2026) has 8 tables and NO heritage_anchor table — the H345 heritage-witness ingest is absent from the current build; re-run it if the heritage witness is needed.
datasets.rebuild: Regenerable (hours): scripts/build_crosswalk.py → build_entries.py → build_forms.py → build_db.py; raw inputs auto-downloaded (see kosha-raw-sqlite row).

datasets.title: DCS 2026 full corpus database
datasets.keying: 5,688,416 tokens · 754,726 sentences · 180,176 lemmas · 270 texts
datasets.notes: DCS upstream is CC BY 4.0; regenerable from CoNLL-U but the ingest is the canonical one — 5 repos historically re-parsed. P-D2/H233.
datasets.rebuild: Fully regenerable: python src/DCS-data-2026/import_dcs_conllu.py over the DCS CoNLL-U dump (upstream CC BY 4.0, redownloadable).

datasets.title: SamudraManthanam Sa↔Ru verse-parallel corpus
datasets.keying: 580,552 corpus lines · 152 sources, verse-aligned (FTS5 index included)
datasets.notes: RIGHTS: contains published RU translations — restricted. H691: NOT single-copy after all — source jsonl are git-tracked in the repo and the DB regenerates from them; census backup-gap row corrected. Was 574,939 lines/148 sources on 06-07-2026, 580,552/152 on 11-07-2026. P-D2/H233.
datasets.rebuild: Regenerable: python web/ingest/ingest.py over git-tracked web/corpus_builder/jsonl/ (WAL empty at verification — counts are live).

datasets.title: Sanskrit Heritage (INRIA) local mirror
datasets.keying: Heritage entry anchors; VH↔SLP1 bridge validated
datasets.notes: LGPLLR rights @DECIDE pending — restricted until ruled. Upstream mirror exists on GitHub, so no backup urgency.

datasets.title: Böhtlingk Indische Sprüche subhāṣitas
datasets.keying: per-saying records, public domain
datasets.notes: Already tracked+public in its repo; listed for discovery, not re-released.

datasets.title: DCS akshara/varṇa/ligature frequency tables
datasets.keying: akshara 7,347 · varṇa 48 · conjunct 999; corpus-wide + per DCS time-slot
datasets.notes: Already tracked+public; listed for discovery.

datasets.title: Consonant series (varga) by DCS period + Cramér's V
datasets.keying: 5 vargas × 5 DCS time-slots; column shares + counts + Δ(I→V)
datasets.notes: Aggregation of dcs-grapheme-frequency; Cramér's V=0.037 → series composition near-stable across ~2kyr; corrects the 2014 П9 claim. Includes slot_era_map.csv.

datasets.title: DCS syntagmatic table — all corpus lemmas (Приложение 7)
datasets.keying: lemma; total occurrences; co-occurrence count; ranked collocate list per lemma
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291: a second, byte-different copy of the same data lives at derived-data/Sinonimy/S_P_D_F/Синтагматическая таблица DCS.csv (UTF-16LE, tab-delimited, Cyrillic header, 47 MB, 82,801 lines incl. blank separator rows) — same 'appendix table' family as this file, same underlying content verified by row-1 sample diff, both created 2026-06-05. This (semicolon-delimited, UTF-8, no BOM, 'Приложение 7' appendix numbering matching the Приложение-6 family below) is registered as canonical; the Sinonimy copy is left in place on disk as a raw/interim export, not separately registered.

datasets.title: DCS parallel-passage alignments, full-size export (PARA/Polnorazmernye)
datasets.keying: one file per source-passage id (`<id>_<n>--<m>.csv`), 245 files; each row = a candidate parallel passage with a GOOD/PARTLY/… match verdict
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291: a same-file-count, same-naming sibling directory PARA/Polnorazmernye-2022-archive/ (245 files, 56 MB) is a superseded 2022 snapshot — sample diff on 104_1--20.csv shows the archive uses a flat '!' match flag where the live version has richer PARTLY/+term-delta annotations. Archive left on disk as a historical snapshot, not separately registered.

datasets.title: DCS verb roots grouped by class (with attested forms)
datasets.keying: root,count — one file per verb class (1.csv…10.csv), roots that have attested corpus forms
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 2 bundle: 10 small per-class files registered as one row per MG ruling (bundle-worthy fragmented candidates → one row, not left as separate small files).

datasets.title: DCS verb-form frequency by class, with prefixed forms
datasets.keying: root;count — one file per verb class (1.csv…10.csv), includes prefixed verb forms
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 2 bundle: 10 small per-class files registered as one row per MG ruling. Sibling of dcs-verb-roots-by-class (same class-numbering scheme, prefixed-forms variant).

datasets.title: DCS syntagmatic tables of frequent lexical cores, by historical period (Приложение 6)
datasets.keying: lemma; frequency; collocates — one file per historical period (1.csv…7.csv)
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 2 bundle: 7 per-period files registered as one row per MG ruling. Same 'appendix table' family as dcs-sintagmatic-appendix7 (Приложение 7 = all-corpus version; this is the per-period breakdown, Приложение 6).

datasets.title: DCS parallel-passage alignments, 'stop-word' variant export (PARA/Stopovye)
datasets.keying: one file per source-passage id, same naming scheme as dcs-parallel-passages-full; includes a split 7z archive member
datasets.source_path: derived-data/Paralleli-v-tekstah-korpusa-SRC/PARA/Stopovye/ (tracked, 113 objects incl. 1 split .7z, *.csv per source passage)
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291: the largest single derived-data bundle in VisualDCS (1.17 GB) — likely a stop-word-filtered variant of dcs-parallel-passages-full, not yet content-diffed against it. Git-tracked (confirmed 07-07-2026), so NOT a backup-risk giant; cross-referenced in Uprava/DATA_LAYERS_CENSUS.md § Actionable gaps instead of the unbackuped-giants table. rows left null: format is per-file alignment records, not a flat table — a meaningful row count needs a schema-aware parse, not a line count.

datasets.title: RussianTranslation raw/working translation-source ingests (all *.jsonl except corpus_lexicon)
datasets.keying: per-source *.renou*/assembled_cards*/dictionary-name jsonl files; formats and schemas vary by pipeline stage
datasets.source_path: RussianTranslation/src/ (GITIGNORED, 55 files — all *.jsonl in this dir except corpus_lexicon.jsonl)
datasets.builder: RussianTranslation/src/_build_*.py pipeline scripts (per-stage: renou alignment, card assembly, quarantine passes)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 3, MG ruling: register as tier=intermediate rather than skip/route to DATA_LAYERS_CENSUS.md — this is the raw/working directory that ALSO produces the already-registered corpus-lexicon dataset (corpus_lexicon.jsonl itself, excluded here to avoid double-counting). Includes progressive pipeline stages (assembled_cards.jsonl → .chunk/.perf/.quarantine/.smoke/.test variants) not yet deduped — see DATA_LAYERS_CENSUS.md § Actionable gaps.

datasets.title: DCS verb-form example-ID lookup table
datasets.keying: single-column list of corpus example/token IDs referenced by other Glagolnye-formy derived assets
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 3, MG ruling: register as tier=intermediate rather than skip — raw example-ID lookup table, not a standalone citable asset in its own right.

datasets.title: Heritage (INRIA) form-level crosswalk extras (gloss, disagreements, forms-only)
datasets.keying: heritage_dico_gloss (mw_key1 → Heritage anchor + FR gloss), heritage_forms_oracle_disagreements (form → Heritage/kosha lemma disagreement class), heritage_only_forms (form → lemma, Heritage-only coverage)
datasets.builder: H099 Heritage mirror pipeline (same builder as mw-heritage-crosswalk)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 4: sibling of mw-heritage-crosswalk (same HeadwordLists dir, same Heritage mirror pipeline) — registered as tier=restricted for the same Heritage LGPLLR-pending rights reason as that entry.

datasets.title: DCS verb-form frequency in corpus (preliminary)
datasets.keying: verb form; frequency count, corpus-wide
datasets.source_path: derived-data/Glagolnye-formy/Частота появления глагольных форм в корпусе (Предварительные данные).csv (tracked)
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 4: same 'derived-data/Fonetika family of small frequency tables as the already-public dcs-grapheme-frequency; filename marks it explicitly 'preliminary data' (Предварительные данные).

datasets.title: DCS compound (samāsa) dictionary derivation
datasets.keying: CompDic.csv (compound headword list, 37,333), cmps.csv (per-compound member breakdown, 401,478), names.csv (168,880 attested compounds w/ frequency vector, already noted in DATA_LAYERS_CENSUS.md), parts.csv (2,667), verbx.csv (3,400, verbal compounds); cmp400000.csv is present but empty (0 bytes)
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 4: bundle of all *.csv in derived-data/Kompozity/ (the dir also holds non-csv working files — .xlsx/.doc/.ods/.7z — out of scope for this manifest, which tracks the csv exports).

datasets.title: Sanskrit-stem co-occurrence table (full)
datasets.keying: stem-pair co-occurrence rows, ID range 1–222342
datasets.builder: unknown (pre-2026 dump; see source_repo git history)
datasets.notes: H291 Step 4: checked for an older non-'NEW' sibling per the open question in this handoff — none found (Sochetaemost-sanskritskih-osnov/ has period-bucketed .txt files under Sochetaemost/ and a separate Ishodnye-dannye/ raw-inputs dir, neither is a prior version of this file); 'NEW' reads as this file's permanent name, not a superseded/current pair.

datasets.title: Which-dictionary routing shared-task benchmark (v1.0)
datasets.keying: scenario id (G-01..G-18 dev, P-01..P-06 test) → gold + accepted CDSL dictionary codes over a 44-code answer space
datasets.notes: H281 (07-07-2026): public benchmark for the routing shared task — CC-BY-SA-4.0 (inherited from the gold panel, declared in-file), single-annotator gold (Fable 5 claude-fable-5, single pass — κ pending a human second pass), open golds / self-reported leaderboard. Data card: csl-guides /developers/data-cards.

datasets.title: Correction loci — locus-resolved correction records across all csl-corrections change files (v1.0)
datasets.keying: (dict, L, k1) locus per correction record; line numbers are batch-time only, never a join key
datasets.source_path: data/derived/correction_loci.tsv (tracked; derived from all batch_*/batches/* change files)
consumers: csl-atlas correction feed (loci heatmap + radar axes, memo backlog #2)
datasets.notes: H294 (07-07-2026): memo docs/HYPOTHESES_AND_VIZ_MEMO.md §5.1 layer. 39,540 records = 39,536 old→new + 4 old→del; process∈{bulk,human} splits the two machine markup batches (BOR 21,990 + LRV/markhom 8,063 = 76%) from human correction. Corrector identity deliberately excluded — public tier. PR: https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/pull/291

datasets.title: CDSL headword ↔ DCS corpus concordance (B1, concordance core)
datasets.keying: union-headwords SLP1 key ↔ DCS lemma_id; canonical concordance-core schema (anchor_type/anchor_id/anchor_key_slp1/corpus_locus/match_method/confidence/evidence_count); ASSERTED tiers xref 12,836 · exact 61,373 · floor 311; the lossy relaxed tier (2,171) is QUARANTINED to dict_corpus_relaxed_candidates.tsv after the golden sample found 3/3 semantically wrong — per-tier table in data/concordance/BUILD_REPORT.md
datasets.notes: Q1 of CONCORDANCE_ROADMAP.md (concordance core: schema + tiered matcher + citable loci, reused Q2-Q4). Coverage sidecar dict_corpus_coverage.tsv (323425 rows): every union headword attested (66,257 = 20.5%) or classed absence — the 20.5% is the honest Zipf reality of a 5.7M-token corpus vs a 323k-headword union. Inputs consumed, never re-derived: dcs-cdsl-xref, union-headwords, dcs-full-sqlite. Citable loci are host-independent dcs:<sent_id> IDs (RISKS R5). DCS is CC BY 4.0; this derivative ships BY-SA with the public tier.

datasets.title: Sundarakāṇḍa two-tier commentary apparatus (Leonov LP volume)
datasets.keying: vulgate verse address 5.<sarga>.<verse> (southern vulgate, 68 sargas)
datasets.source_path: data/sundara_commentary_to_add.json (897 tier-2 notes) + data/leonov_own_notes.json (1058 tier-1) + data/sundara_decision_ledger.json (audit trail); per-chapter layers in data/lexical/, data/analysis/
datasets.notes: RIGHTS: tier-1 notes + verse anchors belong to Leonov/Kostina UNPUBLISHED translation — restricted until the LP volume ships (~08-2026); Sanskrit commentaries source = Gita Supersite, CC BY 4.0. Every tier-2 note carries review_required until both human gates pass (docs/MANUAL.md; role guides H497/H517/H533). Snapshot 10-07-2026, pre-gate counts.

datasets.title: Markup-tag frequency census (44 Cologne v02 dicts)
datasets.keying: dict code x tag -> count + per-1,000-entry rate (96 distinct tags, 1,496,157 entries)
datasets.notes: H683 (11-07-2026, Fable 5 claude-fable-5). Verdicts in data/MARKUP_TAG_CENSUS_CSLORIG_2026.md: MW72/ARMH/FRI/NMMB under-marked; SKD/VCP expected-zero by construction; trace-tag inconsistencies flagged (SCH <ab> x2 in 29k entries, WIL <ls> x6 in 45k).

datasets.title: Sanskrit lexical semantic change scores (PPMI-cosine, DCS 5-slot chronology)
datasets.keying: IAST + SLP1 lemma; per-slot counts, cosine distances (5 slot pairs), freq-shift baseline, graded rank + binary flag (Vedic→Epic)
datasets.builder: build_lsc_pilot.py over sibling dcs-conllu + chapter-info.xml dcsTimeSlot (deterministic, --selftest)
datasets.notes: First Sanskrit LSC derivation (H728, 11-07-2026); freq-shift control Spearman -0.013; binary flag is a q75 heuristic pending ChiWUG-style gold over the 60-lemma target set.

datasets.title: DCS parallel-passage stop-word run, queryable DB (M9 D2)
datasets.keying: parallels 40,573,260 (method='stopword', run='2022-partial', structured columns only, no verse text) + parallel_text 102 + text_names 128 + _id_fullname 106
datasets.rebuild: Fully regenerable: python import_archive.py stopword — reassembles 11 7z-split CSVs from the git-tracked Stopovye/ export (= stopovye-parallel-passages row) and reloads ~40.5M rows. Deliberately NOT shipped (20-30x everything else); the compact sibling archive.sqlite ships as Release asset archive-2026-07.
datasets.notes: H691: the census §2 'uncounted 11 GB blind spot' resolved — live counts match the M9 validation report (reports/m9_archive_ingest.md) exactly. Filter quality='GOOD' for the high-precision subset. The full 245-text stop-word run was deliberately deleted by MG 02-07-2026; only this 113-text partial survives.

datasets.title: SamudraManthanam offline app packs (base + dict)
datasets.keying: base.db 320,902 corpus lines / 146 sources (corpus minus MW+Apte) + dict.db 254,037 lines / 2 sources (MW+Apte); FTS5 + pack_meta/pack_sources
datasets.rebuild: Regenerable from corpus.db (samudra-corpus-db row) via python web/scripts/build_offline_pack.py. Both sha256 values verified against the existing .sha256 sidecar files — match.
datasets.notes: H691: packs are a 13-06-2026 snapshot of corpus.db (574,939 lines = the census's 06-07 corpus count; corpus.db has since grown to 580,552) — stale vs live corpus but internally consistent. Same published-RU-translations rights caveat as samudra-corpus-db.

datasets.title: kosha raw per-dict SQLite inputs (mw, pwg, ap90)
datasets.keying: mw 286,525 · pwg 123,366 · ap90 34,882 rows (one table per dict: key, lnum, data) — sums to kosha-db's 444,773 entries
datasets.rebuild: Re-fetchable, zero risk: scripts/build_entries.py re-downloads the sanskrit-lexicon csl-sqlite release named in RELEASE_TAG.txt; csl-orig commit is cross-dated at build time (see build_entries.py header).
datasets.notes: H691: registered so the census §2 'rebuildable from csl-orig' guess is now a verified recipe with checksums. Not a backup-risk asset.
